summary: Scorching Truckin' and soaring Big River‑Playin' jam
blurb: The show bursts with high energy, highlighted by a blistering Truckin', incendiary China > Rider, and an expansive Big River into Playing In The Band. Jerry’s smoky guitar work and Keith’s on‑fire piano drive the set, making it a must‑listen for 1973 fans.
review: October 27, 1973 at Indianapolis State Fair Coliseum captures the Dead in top form. From the opening of The Promised Land through a rousing first set, Jerry Garcia’s “smoking” guitar tone and emotive vocals—especially on Sugaree—set a fiery tone. Keith Godchaux delivers extraordinary piano solos, notably on Beat It On Down The Line, and his organ work shines throughout. The second set is the real showcase: a scorching Truckin' rolls into an incendiary China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, while the Big River → Playing In The Band sequence blossoms into a soaring jam that feels both country‑heavy and rock‑charged. Loose Lucy, Wharf Rat > Stella Blue, and a smoking rendition of Greatest Story Ever Told add depth and variety, with the crowd feeding off the band’s enthusiasm. Despite occasional drop‑outs in the matrix source, the soundboard‑derived recording preserves the clarity of these moments, making this show one of the season’s standouts.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings praise the performance (scorching Truckin', incendiary China>Rider, soaring Big River‑Playin' jam, country‑flavored set) while the third offers no substantive criticism of the music—its negativity is limited to unknown recording quality. The overall sentiment is strongly positive.

best_recording.reason: Matrix soundboard source with good overall clarity, captures the highlighted jams and delivers the most complete listening experience.

key_highlights: Scorching Truckin' that ignites the second set
Incendiary China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider transition
Big River into Playing In The Band jam that soars
Country‑flavored moments with Loose Lucy, Wharf Rat > Stella Blue and a smoking Greatest Story Ever Told

band_performance.Jerry: Jerry delivered a scorching, smoky guitar tone throughout, with emotive vocals especially on Sugaree.
band_performance.Keith: Keith's piano was on fire, delivering extraordinary solos especially on Beat It On Down The Line and throughout the set.
